page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The configuration includes 32 dedicated vCPUs, representing 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and a 105 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong performance in single-core and multi-core operations. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2074.0 and a multi-core score of 15951.0, both falling into the strong category. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 33.6 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression registered at 13.85 GB/sec. For cryptographic workloads, the Passmark CPU encryption test scored a strong 35678.6, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES and VAES. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, text generation reached a strong 286.26 tokens/sec, while medium 7B model text generation operated at an average rate of 18.95 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the c7i-flex.8xlarge offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require the full resource allocation of standard c7i instances. However, tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring external network storage attachments, and a moderate baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The 2.0 GB of RAM per core ratio also limits its applicability for memory-intensive databases. This server is best suited for compute-bound tasks such as web serving, software compilation, image processing, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 3.2 GHz, it provides 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The server lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-core and multi-core processing, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15951.0 and a static web serving throughput of 51.12 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptographic encryption and single-threaded compression. The c7i-flex.8xlarge is designed as a cost-efficient option for compute-intensive workloads, though it presents tradeoffs in memory density and local storage availability. It is suited for software compilation, web serving, and text or image processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is an Intel-powered, compute-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 6 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for processing-heavy workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong benchmark performance in single-core operations and cryptographic encryption
No local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and image processing
